lead_headline: Foreclosure filings more than doubled week-over-week to 49, led by an $8.1M Arapahoe County commercial property, while restaurant activity cooled sharply from last week's churn.

restaurant.blurb: 18 openings, 16 closures in the past seven days — a marked slowdown from the prior week's 28-47 split. Activity flatlined today with zero movement. Notable: Reunion Bread opened in Platt Park, Takiza Taco shuttered in Union Station, and a Quiznos in Thornton appears caught in listing limbo. The 30-day tally now stands at 36 opens against 61 closures, suggesting continued consolidation.

foreclosure.blurb: 49 new filings published this week, up 145% from last week's 20, totaling $22.2M in defaulted debt. The headline: a $8.1M filing at 7380 S. Clinton in Arapahoe County, the only case above $2M. With 50 auctions scheduled in the next seven days and 1,539 active cases statewide, the foreclosure pipeline is thickening heading into spring. Weld and El Paso counties lead volume at 285 and 250 cases respectively.

retail.blurb: Retail lease tracking in development. Will monitor new tenants, vacancies, and lease rates across metro corridors.

revenue.blurb: Revenue data tool planned to track public company filings and private business performance indicators.

reputation.blurb: Reputation monitoring tool in planning stages to aggregate review trends and sentiment analysis.
